extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property requires that flipping focal_weakness from false to true (all else equal) never lowers acuity. focal_weakness appears in no rule condition except as one conjunct of r8. r8 (priority 100, the highest) fires only when ALL 13 inputs are true and sets acuity := self_care (the LOWEST acuity).

Counterexample baseline: all inputs true EXCEPT focal_weakness = false. With focal_weakness=false, r8 does NOT fire (its conjunction is unsatisfied). The matching rules then include several priority-3 rules — r2 (slurred_speech→emergent), r5 (dyspnea→emergent), r7 (dizziness→emergent), r11 (syncope→emergent), r12 (sweating→emergent). The highest-priority matching rules are priority 3, all assigning emergent, so acuity = emergent.

Now set focal_weakness=true (the witness, all 13 inputs true). r8's full conjunction is now satisfied; with priority 100 it dominates every other rule and assigns acuity := self_care.

Thus raising focal_weakness from false to true lowered acuity from emergent to self_care, violating monotonicity. The witness_assignment is the focal_weakness=true input (all inputs true); the comparison point is the same assignment with focal_weakness=false.
